modules.CausalSmith.Stat.STAT_DiscreteAteMinimax:
Clause (v) is deliberately represented by this scope note rather than a Lean
proposition: every constant below is pointwise in a fixed `epsilon`.  Nothing in
this theorem asserts a matching lower envelope for triangular arrays
`epsilon = epsilon_n`.

Probability and finite-sample algebra used by the ratio branch.  These
lemmas are deliberately stated independently of the calibrated estimator, so
that the handling of an empty empirical treatment arm can be reused.

entries.statement: {d : ℕ} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.PotentialLaw d → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Finite conditional-independence identity `(Y(0),Y(1)) ⟂ A | X`. -/
def ConditionalExchangeability {d : ℕ} (Q : PotentialLaw d) : Prop :=
  ∀ k a y0 y1,
    poAtom Q k a y0 y1 *
        (∑ a' : Bool, ∑ y0' : Bool, ∑ y1' : Bool, poAtom Q k a' y0' y1') =
      (∑ a' : Bool, poAtom Q k a' y0 y1) *
        (∑ y0' : Bool, ∑ y1' : Bool, poAtom Q k a y0' y1')
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ConditionalExchangeability
entries.doc: Finite conditional-independence identity `(Y(0),Y(1)) ⟂ A | X`.

/-- The control-zero subclass `D₀`: overlap laws with `mu_0k=0` in every cell. -/
structure ControlZeroClass (n : ℕ) {d : ℕ} (epsilon : ℝ)
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) : Prop
    extends ExperimentClass n epsilon P (productLaw P n) where
  control_zero : ∀ k, outcomeMean P false k = 0
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ControlZeroClass
entries.doc: The control-zero subclass `D₀`: overlap laws with `mu_0k=0` in every cell.

    (P : DiscreteLaw d) : Prop

  control_zero : ∀ k, outcomeMean P false k = 0

def ControlZeroLaw (n d : ℕ) (epsilon : ℝ) :=
  {P : DiscreteLaw d // ControlZeroClass n epsilon P}

entries.statement: (n : ℕ) → {d : ℕ} → ℝ → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d → MeasureTheory.Measure (Fin n → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d) → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Membership of a sample law in the unrestricted overlap experiment class. -/
structure ExperimentClass (n : ℕ) {d : ℕ} (epsilon : ℝ)
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)) : Prop where
  epsilon_pos : 0 < epsilon
    -- @realizes epsilon(strictly positive overlap constant)
  epsilon_le_half : epsilon ≤ 1 / 2
    -- @realizes epsilon(overlap constant at most 1/2)
  product_law : IidSampling P mu_n
    -- @realizes \mathcal E_{n,d,\epsilon}(member law is productLaw P n)
    -- @realizes O_i(i.i.d. observations governed by the product law)
  overlap : Overlap epsilon P
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ExperimentClass
entries.doc: Membership of a sample law in the unrestricted overlap experiment class.

entries.statement: ℕ → Type
entries.source:
/-- Inputs available to the arithmetic realization: the eight split counts for
each category.  All dependence of the hybrid on the observed sample factors
through this finite vector. -/
abbrev HybridCountInput (d : ℕ) := Fin 2 × Fin d × Fin 2 × Fin 2
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.HybridCountInput
entries.doc:
Inputs available to the arithmetic realization: the eight split counts for
each category.  All dependence of the hybrid on the observed sample factors
through this finite vector.

entries.statement: Prop
entries.source:
/-- Clause (i)'s computability assertion, in the real-arithmetic model used in
the note.  A universal operation-count constant works for every `n,d`; the
program reads only split counts, returns the stated hybrid exactly, and has
`O(d M(n)^4)` arithmetic/comparison operations. -/
def HybridEstimatorComputable : Prop :=
  ∃ K : ℕ, 0 < K ∧
    ∀ n d : ℕ, ∃ program : RealArithmeticProgram (HybridCountInput d),
      program.operationCount ≤ K * d * polynomialDegree n ^ 4 ∧
      ∀ sample : Fin n → Obs d,
        program.eval (hybridCountVector sample) = hybridEstimator sample
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.HybridEstimatorComputable
entries.doc:
Clause (i)'s computability assertion, in the real-arithmetic model used in
the note.  A universal operation-count constant works for every `n,d`; the
program reads only split counts, returns the stated hybrid exactly, and has
`O(d M(n)^4)` arithmetic/comparison operations.

entries.statement: {d n : ℕ} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d → MeasureTheory.Measure (Fin n → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d) → Prop
entries.source:
/-- The observed sample law is the `n`-fold product of the one-unit law. -/
def IidSampling {d n : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (mu_n : Measure (Fin n → Obs d)) : Prop :=
  mu_n = productLaw P n
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.IidSampling
entries.doc: The observed sample law is the `n`-fold product of the one-unit law.

entries.statement: {d : ℕ} → ℝ → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d → Prop
entries.source:
/-- Positive-mass categories have propensity in `[epsilon,1-epsilon]`. -/
def Overlap {d : ℕ} (epsilon : ℝ) (P : DiscreteLaw d) : Prop :=
  ∀ k, 0 < cellMass P k →
    epsilon ≤ propensity P k ∧ propensity P k ≤ 1 - epsilon
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Overlap
entries.doc: Positive-mass categories have propensity in `[epsilon,1-epsilon]`.

abbrev OverlapChoice (r s : A → ℕ) :=

entries.statement: Type u_1 → Type u_1
entries.source:
/-- Syntax trees over exactly the operations admitted by
`RealArithmeticInstruction`. -/
inductive RealArithmeticExpression (ι : Type*) where
  | input (i : ι)
  | const (x : ℝ)
  | add (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | sub (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | mul (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | div (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | branchNonpos (test yes no : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression
entries.doc:
Syntax trees over exactly the operations admitted by
`RealArithmeticInstruction`.

entries.statement: {ι : Type u_1} → ℕ → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι → List (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction ι)
entries.source:
/-- Compile an expression after `offset` already occupied registers. -/
def codeAt {ι : Type*} (offset : ℕ) :
    RealArithmeticExpression ι → List (RealArithmeticInstruction ι)
  | .input i => [.input i]
  | .const x => [.const x]
  | .add x y => codeAt offset x ++ codeAt (offset + nodeCount x) y ++
      [.add (offset + nodeCount x - 1) (offset + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]
  | .sub x y => codeAt offset x ++ codeAt (offset + nodeCount x) y ++
      [.sub (offset + nodeCount x - 1) (offset + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]
  | .mul x y => codeAt offset x ++ codeAt (offset + nodeCount x) y ++
      [.mul (offset + nodeCount x - 1) (offset + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]
  | .div x y => codeAt offset x ++ codeAt (offset + nodeCount x) y ++
      [.div (offset + nodeCount x - 1) (offset + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]
  | .branchNonpos t y n =>
      codeAt offset t ++ codeAt (offset + nodeCount t) y ++
      codeAt (offset + nodeCount t + nodeCount y) n ++
      [.branchNonpos (offset + nodeCount t - 1)
        (offset + nodeCount t + nodeCount y - 1)
        (offset + nodeCount t + nodeCount y + nodeCount n - 1)]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt
entries.doc: Compile an expression after `offset` already occupied registers.

entries.statement:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} (offset : ℕ) (x : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι),
  CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset x =
    match x with
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.input i => [CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction.input i]
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.const x => [CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction.const x]
    | x.add y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset x ++ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt (offset + x.nodeCount) y ++ [CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction.add (offset + x.nodeCount - 1) (offset + x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount - 1)]
    | x.sub y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset x ++ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt (offset + x.nodeCount) y ++ [CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction.sub (offset + x.nodeCount - 1) (offset + x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount - 1)]
    | x.mul y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset x ++ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt (offset + x.nodeCount) y ++ [CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction.mul (offset + x.nodeCount - 1) (offset + x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount - 1)]
    | x.div y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset x ++ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt (offset + x.nodeCount) y ++ [CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction.div (offset + x.nodeCount - 1) (offset + x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount - 1)]
    | t.branchNonpos y n => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset t ++ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt (offset + t.nodeCount) y ++ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt (offset + t.nodeCount + y.nodeCount) n ++ [CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction.branchNonpos (offset + t.nodeCount - 1) (offset + t.nodeCount + y.nodeCount - 1) (offset + t.nodeCount + y.nodeCount + n.nodeCount - 1)]
entries.source:
/-- Compiling an input expression at a given register offset produces the one-instruction program
that reads that input into the next register. -/
add_decl_doc CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt.eq_def
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt.eq_def
entries.doc:
Compiling an input expression at a given register offset produces the one-instruction program
that reads that input into the next register.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ) (e : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι), (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom input registers (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt registers.length e)).getD (registers.length + e.nodeCount - 1) 0 = CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input e
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of code At correct in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma codeAt_correct {ι : Type*} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ)
    (e : RealArithmeticExpression ι) :
    (runFrom input registers (codeAt registers.length e)).getD
      (registers.length + nodeCount e - 1) 0 = eval input e := by
  induction e generalizing registers with
  | input i => simp [codeAt, nodeCount, runFrom, eval,
      RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue]
  | const x => simp [codeAt, nodeCount, runFrom, eval,
      RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue]
  | add x y ihx ihy =>
      rw [codeAt, runFrom_append, runFrom_append]
      let rx := runFrom input registers (codeAt registers.length x)
      have hrx : rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x := by simp [rx]
      let ry := runFrom input rx (codeAt (registers.length + nodeCount x) y)
      have hry : ry.length = registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y := by
        simp [ry, hrx]
      change (runFrom input ry [RealArithmeticInstruction.add
        (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1)
        (registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]).getD _ 0 = _
      simp only [runFrom, List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil]
      simp only [nodeCount, eval]
      rw [show registers.length + (nodeCount x + nodeCount y + 1) - 1 = ry.length by omega]
      rw [getD_append_singleton_length]
      simp only [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue]
      have hx := ihx registers
      have hy := ihy rx
      rw [show rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x from hrx] at hy
      have hx' : ry.getD (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1) 0 = eval input x := by
        have hpos := nodeCount_pos x
        rw [runFrom_getD_of_lt input rx _ _ (by rw [hrx]; omega)]
        exact hx
      exact congrArg₂ (· + ·) hx' hy
  | sub x y ihx ihy =>
      rw [codeAt, runFrom_append, runFrom_append]
      let rx := runFrom input registers (codeAt registers.length x)
      have hrx : rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x := by simp [rx]
      let ry := runFrom input rx (codeAt (registers.length + nodeCount x) y)
      have hry : ry.length = registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y := by
        simp [ry, hrx]
      change (runFrom input ry [RealArithmeticInstruction.sub
        (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1)
        (registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]).getD _ 0 = _
      simp only [runFrom, List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil]
      simp only [nodeCount, eval]
      rw [show registers.length + (nodeCount x + nodeCount y + 1) - 1 = ry.length by omega]
      rw [getD_append_singleton_length]
      simp only [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue]
      have hx := ihx registers
      have hy := ihy rx
      rw [show rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x from hrx] at hy
      have hx' : ry.getD (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1) 0 = eval input x := by
        have hpos := nodeCount_pos x
        rw [runFrom_getD_of_lt input rx _ _ (by rw [hrx]; omega)]
        exact hx
      exact congrArg₂ (· - ·) hx' hy
  | mul x y ihx ihy =>
      rw [codeAt, runFrom_append, runFrom_append]
      let rx := runFrom input registers (codeAt registers.length x)
      have hrx : rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x := by simp [rx]
      let ry := runFrom input rx (codeAt (registers.length + nodeCount x) y)
      have hry : ry.length = registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y := by
        simp [ry, hrx]
      change (runFrom input ry [RealArithmeticInstruction.mul
        (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1)
        (registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]).getD _ 0 = _
      simp only [runFrom, List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil]
      simp only [nodeCount, eval]
      rw [show registers.length + (nodeCount x + nodeCount y + 1) - 1 = ry.length by omega]
      rw [getD_append_singleton_length]
      simp only [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue]
      have hx := ihx registers
      have hy := ihy rx
      rw [show rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x from hrx] at hy
      have hx' : ry.getD (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1) 0 = eval input x := by
        have hpos := nodeCount_pos x
        rw [runFrom_getD_of_lt input rx _ _ (by rw [hrx]; omega)]
        exact hx
      exact congrArg₂ (· * ·) hx' hy
  | div x y ihx ihy =>
      rw [codeAt, runFrom_append, runFrom_append]
      let rx := runFrom input registers (codeAt registers.length x)
      have hrx : rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x := by simp [rx]
      let ry := runFrom input rx (codeAt (registers.length + nodeCount x) y)
      have hry : ry.length = registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y := by
        simp [ry, hrx]
      change (runFrom input ry [RealArithmeticInstruction.div
        (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1)
        (registers.length + nodeCount x + nodeCount y - 1)]).getD _ 0 = _
      simp only [runFrom, List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil]
      simp only [nodeCount, eval]
      rw [show registers.length + (nodeCount x + nodeCount y + 1) - 1 = ry.length by omega]
      rw [getD_append_singleton_length]
      simp only [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue]
      have hx := ihx registers
      have hy := ihy rx
      rw [show rx.length = registers.length + nodeCount x from hrx] at hy
      have hx' : ry.getD (registers.length + nodeCount x - 1) 0 = eval input x := by
        have hpos := nodeCount_pos x
        rw [runFrom_getD_of_lt input rx _ _ (by rw [hrx]; omega)]
        exact hx
      exact congrArg₂ (· / ·) hx' hy
  | branchNonpos t y n iht ihy ihn =>
      rw [codeAt, runFrom_append, runFrom_append, runFrom_append]
      let rt := runFrom input registers (codeAt registers.length t)
      have hrt : rt.length = registers.length + nodeCount t := by simp [rt]
      let ry := runFrom input rt (codeAt (registers.length + nodeCount t) y)
      have hry : ry.length = registers.length + nodeCount t + nodeCount y := by
        simp [ry, hrt]
      let rn := runFrom input ry
        (codeAt (registers.length + nodeCount t + nodeCount y) n)
      have hrn : rn.length =
          registers.length + nodeCount t + nodeCount y + nodeCount n := by
        simp [rn, hry]
      change (runFrom input rn [RealArithmeticInstruction.branchNonpos
        (registers.length + nodeCount t - 1)
        (registers.length + nodeCount t + nodeCount y - 1)
        (registers.length + nodeCount t + nodeCount y + nodeCount n - 1)]).getD _ 0 = _
      simp only [runFrom, List.foldl_cons, List.foldl_nil]
      simp only [nodeCount, eval]
      rw [show registers.length +
          (nodeCount t + nodeCount y + nodeCount n + 1) - 1 = rn.length by omega]
      rw [getD_append_singleton_length]
      simp only [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue]
      have ht := iht registers
      have hy := ihy rt
      have hn := ihn ry
      rw [show rt.length = registers.length + nodeCount t from hrt] at hy
      rw [show ry.length = registers.length + nodeCount t + nodeCount y from hry] at hn
      have ht' : rn.getD (registers.length + nodeCount t - 1) 0 = eval input t := by
        have htpos := nodeCount_pos t
        rw [runFrom_getD_of_lt input ry _ _ (by rw [hry]; omega)]
        rw [runFrom_getD_of_lt input rt _ _ (by rw [hrt]; omega)]
        exact ht
      have hy' : rn.getD (registers.length + nodeCount t + nodeCount y - 1) 0 =
          eval input y := by
        have htpos := nodeCount_pos t
        have hypos := nodeCount_pos y
        rw [runFrom_getD_of_lt input ry _ _ (by rw [hry]; omega)]
        exact hy
      rw [ht', hy', hn]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt_correct
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of code At correct in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: {ι : Type u_1} → (ι → ℝ) → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Syntax trees over exactly the operations admitted by
`RealArithmeticInstruction`. -/
inductive RealArithmeticExpression (ι : Type*) where
  | input (i : ι)
  | const (x : ℝ)
  | add (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | sub (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | mul (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | div (x y : RealArithmeticExpression ι)
  | branchNonpos (test yes no : RealArithmeticExpression ι)

namespace RealArithmeticExpression

noncomputable def eval {ι : Type*} (input : ι → ℝ) : RealArithmeticExpression ι → ℝ
  | .input i => input i
  | .const x => x
  | .add x y => eval input x + eval input y
  | .sub x y => eval input x - eval input y
  | .mul x y => eval input x * eval input y
  | .div x y => eval input x / eval input y
  | .branchNonpos test yes no =>
      if eval input test ≤ 0 then eval input yes else eval input no
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval
entries.doc:
Syntax trees over exactly the operations admitted by
`RealArithmeticInstruction`.

entries.statement:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} (input : ι → ℝ) (x : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι),
  CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input x =
    match x with
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.input i => input i
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.const x => x
    | x.add y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input x + CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input y
    | x.sub y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input x - CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input y
    | x.mul y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input x * CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input y
    | x.div y => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input x / CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input y
    | test.branchNonpos yes no => if CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input test ≤ 0 then CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input yes else CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input no
entries.source:
/-- Evaluating an input expression returns the real number assigned to that input. -/
add_decl_doc CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval.eq_def
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval.eq_def
entries.doc: Evaluating an input expression returns the real number assigned to that input.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (e : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι) (input : ι → ℝ), e.program.eval input = CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval input e
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of eval program in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma eval_program {ι : Type*} (e : RealArithmeticExpression ι) (input : ι → ℝ) :
    (program e).eval input = eval input e := by
  simpa [program, RealArithmeticProgram.eval, RealArithmeticProgram.run, runFrom]
    using codeAt_correct input [] e
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.eval_program
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of eval program in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: ∀ (xs : List ℝ) (x : ℝ), (xs ++ [x]).getD xs.length 0 = x
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated upper bound for get D append singleton length. -/
lemma getD_append_singleton_length (xs : List ℝ) (x : ℝ) :
    (xs ++ [x]).getD xs.length 0 = x := by simp
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.getD_append_singleton_length
entries.doc: Establishes the stated upper bound for get D append singleton length.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (offset : ℕ) (e : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι), (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset e).length = e.nodeCount
entries.source:
/-- The compiled instruction list for an expression has exactly one instruction per node of its expression tree. -/
@[simp] lemma length_codeAt {ι : Type*} (offset : ℕ)
    (e : RealArithmeticExpression ι) : (codeAt offset e).length = nodeCount e := by
  induction e generalizing offset with
  | input i => simp [codeAt, nodeCount]
  | const x => simp [codeAt, nodeCount]
  | add x y ihx ihy => simp [codeAt, nodeCount, ihx, ihy] <;> omega
  | sub x y ihx ihy => simp [codeAt, nodeCount, ihx, ihy] <;> omega
  | mul x y ihx ihy => simp [codeAt, nodeCount, ihx, ihy] <;> omega
  | div x y ihx ihy => simp [codeAt, nodeCount, ihx, ihy] <;> omega
  | branchNonpos t y n iht ihy ihn =>
      simp [codeAt, nodeCount, iht, ihy, ihn] <;> omega
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.length_codeAt
entries.doc: The compiled instruction list for an expression has exactly one instruction per node of its expression tree.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ) (code : List (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction ι)), (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom input registers code).length = registers.length + code.length
entries.source:
/-- Executing a list of instructions appends exactly one register value for each instruction, leaving the initial registers in place. -/
@[simp] lemma length_runFrom {ι : Type*} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ)
    (code : List (RealArithmeticInstruction ι)) :
    (runFrom input registers code).length = registers.length + code.length := by
  induction code generalizing registers with
  | nil => simp [runFrom]
  | cons i code ih =>
      rw [show runFrom input registers (i :: code) =
        runFrom input (registers ++
          [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue input registers i]) code by rfl]
      rw [ih]
      simp
      omega
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.length_runFrom
entries.doc: Executing a list of instructions appends exactly one register value for each instruction, leaving the initial registers in place.

entries.statement: {ι : Type u_1} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- Defines node Count, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator. -/
def nodeCount {ι : Type*} : RealArithmeticExpression ι → ℕ
  | .input _ | .const _ => 1
  | .add x y | .sub x y | .mul x y | .div x y => nodeCount x + nodeCount y + 1
  | .branchNonpos t y n => nodeCount t + nodeCount y + nodeCount n + 1
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.nodeCount
entries.doc: Defines node Count, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator.

entries.statement:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} (x : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι),
  x.nodeCount =
    match x with
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.input i => 1
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.const x => 1
    | x.add y => x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount + 1
    | x.sub y => x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount + 1
    | x.mul y => x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount + 1
    | x.div y => x.nodeCount + y.nodeCount + 1
    | t.branchNonpos y n => t.nodeCount + y.nodeCount + n.nodeCount + 1
entries.source:
/-- An input or constant expression tree contains exactly one node. -/
add_decl_doc CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.nodeCount.eq_def
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.nodeCount.eq_def
entries.doc: An input or constant expression tree contains exactly one node.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (e : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι), 0 < e.nodeCount
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of node Count pos in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma nodeCount_pos {ι : Type*} (e : RealArithmeticExpression ι) : 0 < nodeCount e := by
  cases e <;> simp [nodeCount]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.nodeCount_pos
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of node Count pos in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: {ι : Type u_1} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι → ℕ
entries.source:
/-- Defines operation Count, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator. -/
def operationCount {ι : Type*} : RealArithmeticExpression ι → ℕ
  | .input _ | .const _ => 0
  | .add x y | .sub x y | .mul x y | .div x y =>
      operationCount x + operationCount y + 1
  | .branchNonpos t y n =>
      operationCount t + operationCount y + operationCount n + 1
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.operationCount
entries.doc: Defines operation Count, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator.

entries.statement:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} (x : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι),
  x.operationCount =
    match x with
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.input i => 0
    | CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.const x => 0
    | x.add y => x.operationCount + y.operationCount + 1
    | x.sub y => x.operationCount + y.operationCount + 1
    | x.mul y => x.operationCount + y.operationCount + 1
    | x.div y => x.operationCount + y.operationCount + 1
    | t.branchNonpos y n => t.operationCount + y.operationCount + n.operationCount + 1
entries.source:
/-- An input or constant expression tree requires no arithmetic operations. -/
add_decl_doc CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.operationCount.eq_def
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.operationCount.eq_def
entries.doc: An input or constant expression tree requires no arithmetic operations.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (offset : ℕ) (e : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι), (List.map CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.codeAt offset e)).sum = e.operationCount
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of operation Count code At in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma operationCount_codeAt {ι : Type*} (offset : ℕ)
    (e : RealArithmeticExpression ι) :
    ((codeAt offset e).map RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost).sum = operationCount e := by
  induction e generalizing offset with
  | input i => simp [codeAt, operationCount, RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost]
  | const x => simp [codeAt, operationCount, RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost]
  | add x y ihx ihy =>
      simp [codeAt, operationCount, ihx, ihy, RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost] <;> omega
  | sub x y ihx ihy =>
      simp [codeAt, operationCount, ihx, ihy, RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost] <;> omega
  | mul x y ihx ihy =>
      simp [codeAt, operationCount, ihx, ihy, RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost] <;> omega
  | div x y ihx ihy =>
      simp [codeAt, operationCount, ihx, ihy, RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost] <;> omega
  | branchNonpos t y n iht ihy ihn =>
      simp [codeAt, operationCount, iht, ihy, ihn,
        RealArithmeticProgram.instructionCost] <;> omega
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.operationCount_codeAt
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of operation Count code At in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (e : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι), e.program.operationCount = e.operationCount
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of operation Count program in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma operationCount_program {ι : Type*} (e : RealArithmeticExpression ι) :
    (program e).operationCount = operationCount e := by
  simp [program, RealArithmeticProgram.operationCount, operationCount_codeAt]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.operationCount_program
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of operation Count program in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: {ι : Type u_1} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression ι → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticProgram ι
entries.source:
/-- Defines program, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator. -/
noncomputable def program {ι : Type*} (e : RealArithmeticExpression ι) :
    RealArithmeticProgram ι := (codeAt 0 e, nodeCount e - 1)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.program
entries.doc: Defines program, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator.

entries.statement: {ι : Type u_1} → (ι → ℝ) → List ℝ → List (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction ι) → List ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Defines run From, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator. -/
noncomputable def runFrom {ι : Type*} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ)
    (code : List (RealArithmeticInstruction ι)) : List ℝ :=
  code.foldl (fun rs instruction => rs ++
    [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue input rs instruction]) registers
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom
entries.doc: Defines run From, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ) (xs ys : List (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction ι)), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom input registers (xs ++ ys) = CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom input (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom input registers xs) ys
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of run From append in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma runFrom_append {ι : Type*} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ)
    (xs ys : List (RealArithmeticInstruction ι)) :
    runFrom input registers (xs ++ ys) = runFrom input (runFrom input registers xs) ys := by
  simp [runFrom, List.foldl_append]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom_append
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of run From append in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ) (code : List (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction ι)), ∃ tail, CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom input registers code = registers ++ tail
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated equality relating run From eq append. -/
lemma runFrom_eq_append {ι : Type*} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ)
    (code : List (RealArithmeticInstruction ι)) :
    ∃ tail, runFrom input registers code = registers ++ tail := by
  induction code generalizing registers with
  | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp [runFrom]⟩
  | cons i code ih =>
      rcases ih (registers ++
        [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue input registers i]) with ⟨tail, htail⟩
      refine ⟨RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue input registers i :: tail, ?_⟩
      rw [show runFrom input registers (i :: code) =
        runFrom input (registers ++
          [RealArithmeticProgram.instructionValue input registers i]) code by rfl]
      rw [htail]
      simp
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom_eq_append
entries.doc: Establishes the stated equality relating run From eq append.

entries.statement: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ) (code : List (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction ι)), ∀ i < registers.length, (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom input registers code).getD i 0 = registers.getD i 0
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of run From get D of lt in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma runFrom_getD_of_lt {ι : Type*} (input : ι → ℝ) (registers : List ℝ)
    (code : List (RealArithmeticInstruction ι)) (i : ℕ) (hi : i < registers.length) :
    (runFrom input registers code).getD i 0 = registers.getD i 0 := by
  rcases runFrom_eq_append input registers code with ⟨tail, htail⟩
  rw [htail, List.getD_append _ _ _ _ hi]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression.runFrom_getD_of_lt
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of run From get D of lt in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: Type u_1 → Type u_1
entries.source:
/-- One instruction in a straight-line real-arithmetic program.  Natural
numbers address previously computed registers, allowing factorials and powers
to be computed once and shared across all polynomial monomials. -/
inductive RealArithmeticInstruction (ι : Type*) where
  | input (i : ι)
  | const (x : ℝ)
  | add (x y : ℕ)
  | sub (x y : ℕ)
  | mul (x y : ℕ)
  | div (x y : ℕ)
  | branchNonpos (test yes no : ℕ)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticInstruction
entries.doc:
One instruction in a straight-line real-arithmetic program.  Natural
numbers address previously computed registers, allowing factorials and powers
to be computed once and shared across all polynomial monomials.

entries.statement: Type u_1 → Type (max u_1 0)
entries.source:
/-- A finite instruction list paired with its designated output register. -/
abbrev RealArithmeticProgram (ι : Type*) := List (RealArithmeticInstruction ι) × ℕ
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticProgram
entries.doc: A finite instruction list paired with its designated output register.

entries.statement: ℝ → Prop
entries.source:
/-- **Cited gate (Zeng, Balakrishnan, Han, Kennedy, 2026).**
Source handle `cite:zeng-balakrishnan-han-kennedy-2024`, arXiv:2405.00118v3,
Theorem 2 and Appendix C.5, with the fixed-sample transfer in Lemma 4 and
Appendix D.2.  This is the documented fixed-sample control-zero specialization:
the treated-arm minimax risk has the stated scale for every positive alphabet
size in the source range. -/
def ZengOneArmMinimaxLower (epsilon : ℝ) : Prop :=
  0 < epsilon ∧ epsilon < 1 / 2 →
    ∃ a_epsilon b_epsilon : ℝ, ∃ N_epsilon : ℕ,
      0 < a_epsilon ∧ 0 < b_epsilon ∧
      ∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < d → N_epsilon ≤ n →
        (d : ℝ) ≤ b_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
        a_epsilon * minimaxRate n d ≤ oneArmMinimaxRisk n d epsilon
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ZengOneArmMinimaxLower
entries.doc:
**Cited gate (Zeng, Balakrishnan, Han, Kennedy, 2026).**
Source handle `cite:zeng-balakrishnan-han-kennedy-2024`, arXiv:2405.00118v3,
Theorem 2 and Appendix C.5, with the fixed-sample transfer in Lemma 4 and
Appendix D.2.  This is the documented fixed-sample control-zero specialization:
the treated-arm minimax risk has the stated scale for every positive alphabet
size in the source range.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Overlap epsilon P → ∀ (k : Fin d), |CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellPhi (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellVector P k)| ≤ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated upper bound for abs cell Phi cell Vector le mass. -/
lemma abs_cellPhi_cellVector_le_mass {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (hOverlap : Overlap epsilon P) (k : Fin d) :
    |cellPhi (cellVector P k)| ≤ cellMass P k := by
  by_cases hz : cellVector P k = 0
  · simp [cellPhi, hz, (cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k).1]
  · simp only [cellPhi, hz, if_false]
    rw [vectorMass_cellVector, vectorArmMass_cellVector,
      vectorArmMass_cellVector]
    have hm1 := outcomeMean_mem_unitInterval P true k
    have hm0 := outcomeMean_mem_unitInterval P false k
    have hp := cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k
    rcases hm1 with ⟨hm1lo, hm1hi⟩
    rcases hm0 with ⟨hm0lo, hm0hi⟩
    have hdiff : |outcomeMean P true k - outcomeMean P false k| ≤ 1 := by
      rw [abs_le]
      constructor <;> linarith
    change |cellMass P k *
      (outcomeMean P true k - outcomeMean P false k)| ≤ cellMass P k
    rw [abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg hp.1]
    exact mul_le_of_le_one_right hp.1 hdiff
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.abs_cellPhi_cellVector_le_mass
entries.doc: Establishes the stated upper bound for abs cell Phi cell Vector le mass.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d), |CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellPhi (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellVector P k)| ≤ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated upper bound for abs cell Phi cell Vector le mass unconditional. -/
lemma abs_cellPhi_cellVector_le_mass_unconditional {d : ℕ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) :
    |cellPhi (cellVector P k)| ≤ cellMass P k := by
  rw [cellPhi_cellVector_eq_weighted]
  have hp := cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k
  have hm1 := outcomeMean_mem_unitInterval P true k
  have hm0 := outcomeMean_mem_unitInterval P false k
  rw [abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg hp.1]
  apply mul_le_of_le_one_right hp.1
  rw [abs_le]
  constructor <;> linarith [hm1.1, hm1.2, hm0.1, hm0.2]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.abs_cellPhi_cellVector_le_mass_unconditional
entries.doc: Establishes the stated upper bound for abs cell Phi cell Vector le mass unconditional.

entries.statement: ∀ {n d : ℕ} (sample : Fin n → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d) (k : Fin d), |CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.empiricalRatioCell sample k| ≤ ↑(CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.splitCategoryCount sample 1 k) / ↑(CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.splitSize n 1)
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated upper bound for abs empirical Ratio Cell le. -/
lemma abs_empiricalRatioCell_le {n d : ℕ} (sample : Fin n → Obs d)
    (k : Fin d) :
    |empiricalRatioCell sample k| ≤
      (splitCategoryCount sample 1 k : ℝ) / splitSize n 1 := by
  let r1 : ℝ := (splitCellCount sample 1 k 1 1 : ℝ) /
    (splitCellCount sample 1 k 1 0 + splitCellCount sample 1 k 1 1 : ℕ)
  let r0 : ℝ := (splitCellCount sample 1 k 0 1 : ℝ) /
    (splitCellCount sample 1 k 0 0 + splitCellCount sample 1 k 0 1 : ℕ)
  have hr1 := empirical_arm_ratio_mem_unitInterval sample k 1
  have hr0 := empirical_arm_ratio_mem_unitInterval sample k 0
  rcases hr1 with ⟨hr1lo, hr1hi⟩
  rcases hr0 with ⟨hr0lo, hr0hi⟩
  have hdiff : |r1 - r0| ≤ 1 := by
    rw [abs_le]
    constructor <;> dsimp [r1, r0] at hr1lo hr1hi hr0lo hr0hi ⊢ <;> linarith
  have hweight : 0 ≤
      (splitCategoryCount sample 1 k : ℝ) / splitSize n 1 := by positivity
  change |((splitCategoryCount sample 1 k : ℝ) / splitSize n 1) * (r1 - r0)| ≤ _
  rw [abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg hweight]
  exact mul_le_of_le_one_right hweight hdiff
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.abs_empiricalRatioCell_le
entries.doc: Establishes the stated upper bound for abs empirical Ratio Cell le.

entries.statement: ∀ {J : Type u_3} [inst : Fintype J] [inst_1 : DecidableEq J] {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (z : J → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d) (H : Finset (Fin d)) (a : Fin 2), |CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.fixedMissingOutcomeBias P z H a| ≤ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.fixedMissingCount z H (finTwoEquiv a)
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated upper bound for abs fixed Missing Outcome Bias le. -/
lemma abs_fixedMissingOutcomeBias_le {J : Type*} [Fintype J]
    [DecidableEq J] {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (z : J → Obs d)
    (H : Finset (Fin d)) (a : Fin 2) :
    |fixedMissingOutcomeBias P z H a| ≤
      fixedMissingCount z H (finTwoEquiv a) := by
  classical
  calc
    |fixedMissingOutcomeBias P z H a| ≤
        ∑ k ∈ H, |outcomeMean P (finTwoEquiv a) k *
          (missingIndexCount z (categorySet k)
            (categoryArmSet k (finTwoEquiv a)) : ℝ)| :=
      Finset.abs_sum_le_sum_abs _ _
    _ ≤ ∑ k ∈ H,
        (missingIndexCount z (categorySet k)
          (categoryArmSet k (finTwoEquiv a)) : ℝ) := by
      apply Finset.sum_le_sum
      intro k hk
      have hmu := outcomeMean_mem_unitInterval P (finTwoEquiv a) k
      rw [abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg hmu.1,
        abs_of_nonneg (by positivity :
          0 ≤ (missingIndexCount z (categorySet k)
            (categoryArmSet k (finTwoEquiv a)) : ℝ))]
      exact mul_le_of_le_one_left (by positivity) hmu.2
    _ = fixedMissingCount z H (finTwoEquiv a) := rfl
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.abs_fixedMissingOutcomeBias_le
entries.doc: Establishes the stated upper bound for abs fixed Missing Outcome Bias le.

entries.statement: ∀ {M : ℕ}, 0 < M → ∀ (j : ℕ), |CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.gCoefficient M j| = 2 ^ (2 * j + 3) / (↑M * (↑M + ↑j + 2)) * ↑((M + j + 2).choose (2 * j + 4))
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated property of abs g Coefficient in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction. -/
lemma abs_gCoefficient {M : ℕ} (hM : 0 < M) (j : ℕ) :
    |gCoefficient M j| = 2^(2*j+3) / ((M:ℝ)*(M+j+2)) * Nat.choose (M+j+2) (2*j+4) := by
  unfold gCoefficient
  rw [abs_mul, abs_div, abs_mul, abs_pow, abs_neg, abs_one]
  rw [abs_of_nonneg (by positivity : (0:ℝ) ≤ 2^(2*j+3))]
  rw [abs_of_pos (mul_pos (by exact_mod_cast hM) (by positivity))]
  rw [abs_of_nonneg (by positivity : (0:ℝ) ≤ (Nat.choose (M+j+2) (2*j+4) : ℝ))]
  norm_num
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.abs_gCoefficient
entries.doc: Establishes the stated property of abs g Coefficient in the discrete average-treatment-effect construction.

entries.statement: ∀ {n d : ℕ} (sample : Fin n → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d), |CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.heavyContribution sample| ≤ 1
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated upper bound for abs heavy Contribution le one. -/
lemma abs_heavyContribution_le_one {n d : ℕ} (sample : Fin n → Obs d) :
    |heavyContribution sample| ≤ 1 := by
  calc
    |heavyContribution sample| ≤
        ∑ k ∈ heavyCells sample, |empiricalRatioCell sample k| := by
      exact Finset.abs_sum_le_sum_abs _ _
    _ ≤ ∑ k ∈ heavyCells sample,
        (splitCategoryCount sample 1 k : ℝ) / splitSize n 1 := by
      exact Finset.sum_le_sum fun k _hk => abs_empiricalRatioCell_le sample k
    _ ≤ ∑ k : Fin d,
        (splitCategoryCount sample 1 k : ℝ) / splitSize n 1 := by
      apply Finset.sum_le_sum_of_subset_of_nonneg (Finset.subset_univ _)
      intro k _hk _hnot
      positivity
    _ = (splitSize n 1 : ℝ) / splitSize n 1 := by
      rw [← Finset.sum_div]
      congr 1
      exact_mod_cast sum_splitCategoryCount sample 1
    _ ≤ 1 := div_le_one_of_le₀ le_rfl (by positivity)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.abs_heavyContribution_le_one
entries.doc: Establishes the stated upper bound for abs heavy Contribution le one.

entries.statement: ∀ {n d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (sample : Fin n → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d), |CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.targetHeavy P sample| ≤ 1
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated upper bound for abs target Heavy le one. -/
lemma abs_targetHeavy_le_one {n d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (sample : Fin n → Obs d) : |targetHeavy P sample| ≤ 1 := by
  calc
    |targetHeavy P sample| ≤
        ∑ k ∈ heavyCells sample, |cellPhi (cellVector P k)| :=
      Finset.abs_sum_le_sum_abs _ _
    _ ≤ ∑ k ∈ heavyCells sample, cellMass P k := by
      exact Finset.sum_le_sum fun k _hk ↦
        abs_cellPhi_cellVector_le_mass_unconditional P k
    _ ≤ ∑ k : Fin d, cellMass P k := by
      apply Finset.sum_le_sum_of_subset_of_nonneg (Finset.subset_univ _)
      intro k _hk _hnot
      exact (cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k).1
    _ = 1 := sum_cellMass_eq_one P
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.abs_targetHeavy_le_one
entries.doc: Establishes the stated upper bound for abs target Heavy le one.

entries.statement: ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Falling factorial, reusing Mathlib's `Nat.descFactorial`. -/
def fallingFactorial (z r : ℕ) : ℕ := z.descFactorial r
  -- @realizes z_r(z descending factorial r; z_0=1)

noncomputable def logScale (n : ℕ) : ℝ := Real.log (Real.exp 1 * n)

def calibrationA : ℕ := 6
def lambda0 : ℕ := 256
def b0 : ℕ := 4096

noncomputable def dA : ℝ := 8 * Real.log (27 / 4)

noncomputable def alpha0 : ℝ :=
  min 1 (min (1 / (32 * Real.log 6)) (1 / (256 * dA)))
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.alpha0
entries.doc: Falling factorial, reusing Mathlib's `Nat.descFactorial`.

entries.statement: ℕ → {d : ℕ} → Fin d → Fin 2 → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.RealArithmeticExpression (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.HybridCountInput d)
entries.source:
/-- Defines arm Factorial Expression, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator. -/
noncomputable def armFactorialExpression (n : ℕ) {d : ℕ} (k : Fin d) (a : Fin 2) :
    RealArithmeticExpression (HybridCountInput d) :=
  let M := polynomialDegree n
  let B := bandwidth n
  expressionSum ((List.range (M - 1)).map fun j =>
    expressionSum ((List.range (j + 1)).map fun t =>
      expressionSum (hybridCellList.map fun ay =>
        .mul
          (.const (B⁻¹ * gCoefficient M j * B⁻¹ ^ j * (Nat.choose j t : ℝ)))
          (factorialMonomialExpression (n := n) k (hybridTermIndex a ay j t)))))
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armFactorialExpression
entries.doc: Defines arm Factorial Expression, the stated quantity or construction used in the discrete average-treatment-effect estimator.

entries.statement: {d : ℕ} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d → Fin d → Bool → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- Joint mass `P(X=k,A=a)`. -/
noncomputable def armMass {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) : ℝ :=
  ∑ y : Bool, jointMass P k a y
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass
entries.doc: Joint mass `P(X=k,A=a)`.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass P k false + CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass P k true = CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k
entries.source:
/-- The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero. -/
noncomputable def armPropensity {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) : ℝ :=
  armMass P k a / cellMass P k

lemma armMass_add_eq_cellMass {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) :
    armMass P k false + armMass P k true = cellMass P k := by
  simp [armMass, cellMass]
  ring
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass_add_eq_cellMass
entries.doc: The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) (a : Bool), 0 < CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass P k a = CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k * CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity P k a
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated equality relating arm Mass eq cell Mass mul arm Propensity. -/
lemma armMass_eq_cellMass_mul_armPropensity {d : ℕ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) (a : Bool)
    (hp : 0 < cellMass P k) :
    armMass P k a = cellMass P k * armPropensity P k a := by
  unfold armPropensity
  field_simp
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass_eq_cellMass_mul_armPropensity
entries.doc: Establishes the stated equality relating arm Mass eq cell Mass mul arm Propensity.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) (a : Bool), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass P k a ≤ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k
entries.source:
/-- The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero. -/
noncomputable def armPropensity {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) : ℝ :=
  armMass P k a / cellMass P k

lemma armMass_add_eq_cellMass {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) :
    armMass P k false + armMass P k true = cellMass P k := by
  simp [armMass, cellMass]
  ring

lemma armMass_nonneg {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d)
    (a : Bool) : 0 ≤ armMass P k a := by
  unfold armMass
  exact Finset.sum_nonneg fun y _ ↦ (jointMass_mem_unitInterval P k a y).1

lemma armMass_le_cellMass {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d)
    (a : Bool) : armMass P k a ≤ cellMass P k := by
  rw [← armMass_add_eq_cellMass]
  cases a
  · exact le_add_of_nonneg_right (armMass_nonneg P k true)
  · exact le_add_of_nonneg_left (armMass_nonneg P k false)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass_le_cellMass
entries.doc: The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) (a : Bool), 0 ≤ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass P k a
entries.source:
/-- The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero. -/
noncomputable def armPropensity {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) : ℝ :=
  armMass P k a / cellMass P k

lemma armMass_add_eq_cellMass {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) :
    armMass P k false + armMass P k true = cellMass P k := by
  simp [armMass, cellMass]
  ring

lemma armMass_nonneg {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d)
    (a : Bool) : 0 ≤ armMass P k a := by
  unfold armMass
  exact Finset.sum_nonneg fun y _ ↦ (jointMass_mem_unitInterval P k a y).1
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armMass_nonneg
entries.doc: The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero.

entries.statement: {d : ℕ} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d → Fin d → Fin 2 → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- One-observation outcome residual for a specified category and arm. -/
noncomputable def armOutcomeResidual {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Fin 2) (z : Obs d) : ℝ :=
  if z.1 = k ∧ z.2.1 = finTwoEquiv a then
    (if z.2.2 then 1 else 0) - outcomeMean P (finTwoEquiv a) k
  else 0
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armOutcomeResidual
entries.doc: One-observation outcome residual for a specified category and arm.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k l : Fin d), k ≠ l → ∀ (a : Fin 2) (x : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armOutcomeResidual P k a x * CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armOutcomeResidual P l a x = 0
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated equality relating arm Outcome Residual mul eq zero of ne. -/
lemma armOutcomeResidual_mul_eq_zero_of_ne {d : ℕ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (k l : Fin d) (hkl : k ≠ l) (a : Fin 2)
    (x : Obs d) :
    armOutcomeResidual P k a x * armOutcomeResidual P l a x = 0 := by
  unfold armOutcomeResidual
  by_cases hk : x.1 = k
  · have hl : x.1 ≠ l := fun h => hkl (hk.symm.trans h)
    simp [hk, hkl]
  · simp [hk]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armOutcomeResidual_mul_eq_zero_of_ne
entries.doc: Establishes the stated equality relating arm Outcome Residual mul eq zero of ne.

entries.statement: {d : ℕ} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d → Fin d → Bool → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero. -/
noncomputable def armPropensity {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) : ℝ :=
  armMass P k a / cellMass P k
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity
entries.doc: The arm probability inside a positive-mass category, totalized at zero.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d), 0 < CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity P k false = 1 - CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.propensity P k
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated equality relating arm Propensity eq false. -/
lemma armPropensity_eq_false {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) (hp : 0 < cellMass P k) :
    armPropensity P k false = 1 - propensity P k := by
  have hsum := armMass_add_eq_cellMass P k
  rw [armPropensity, propensity]
  field_simp
  linarith
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity_eq_false
entries.doc: Establishes the stated equality relating arm Propensity eq false.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity P k true = CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.propensity P k
entries.source:
/-- Establishes the stated equality relating arm Propensity eq true. -/
lemma armPropensity_eq_true {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) : armPropensity P k true = propensity P k := rfl
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity_eq_true
entries.doc: Establishes the stated equality relating arm Propensity eq true.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Overlap epsilon P → ∀ (k : Fin d) (a : Bool), 0 < CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k → epsilon ≤ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity P k a
entries.source:
/-- Overlap supplies the same lower bound for either arm probability. -/
lemma armPropensity_lower_of_overlap {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (hOverlap : Overlap epsilon P)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) (hp : 0 < cellMass P k) :
    epsilon ≤ armPropensity P k a := by
  rcases hOverlap k hp with ⟨hlo, hhi⟩
  cases a
  · rw [armPropensity_eq_false P k hp]
    linarith
  · exact hlo
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity_lower_of_overlap
entries.doc: Overlap supplies the same lower bound for either arm probability.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) (k : Fin d) (a : Bool), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity P k a ∈ Set.Icc 0 1
entries.source:
/-- Shows that arm Propensity mem unit Interval lies in the stated set or interval. -/
lemma armPropensity_mem_unitInterval {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) : armPropensity P k a ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := by
  exact ⟨div_nonneg (armMass_nonneg P k a)
      (cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k).1,
    div_le_one_of_le₀ (armMass_le_cellMass P k a)
      (cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k).1⟩
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.armPropensity_mem_unitInterval
entries.doc: Shows that arm Propensity mem unit Interval lies in the stated set or interval.

entries.statement: {d : ℕ} → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d → ℝ
entries.source:
/-- The observed-data ATE functional `sum_k phi(q_k)`. -/
-- @node: def:ate-functional
noncomputable def ateFunctional {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) : ℝ :=
  ∑ k : Fin d, cellPhi (cellVector P k)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional
entries.doc: The observed-data ATE functional `sum_k phi(q_k)`.

entries.statement: ∀ {n d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ControlZeroLaw n d epsilon), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional ↑P = CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.treatedFunctional ↑P
entries.source:
/-- On the control-zero subclass the observed-data ATE is the treated functional. -/
lemma ateFunctional_eq_treated_on_controlZero {n d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ}
    (P : ControlZeroLaw n d epsilon) :
    ateFunctional P.1 = treatedFunctional P.1 := by
  rw [ateFunctional_eq_weighted_regression P.1 P.2.overlap]
  unfold treatedFunctional
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro k _
  rw [P.2.control_zero k, sub_zero]
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional_eq_treated_on_controlZero
entries.doc: On the control-zero subclass the observed-data ATE is the treated functional.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Overlap epsilon P → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional P = ∑ k, CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k * (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.outcomeMean P true k - CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.outcomeMean P false k)
entries.source:
/-- The equivalent weighted-regression formula under overlap. -/
lemma ateFunctional_eq_weighted_regression {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (h : Overlap epsilon P) :
    ateFunctional P =
      ∑ k : Fin d, cellMass P k * (outcomeMean P true k - outcomeMean P false k) := by
  classical
  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
  intro k _
  by_cases hz : cellVector P k = 0
  · have h00 := congrFun hz (0, 0)
    have h01 := congrFun hz (0, 1)
    have h10 := congrFun hz (1, 0)
    have h11 := congrFun hz (1, 1)
    simp [cellVector, finTwoEquiv] at h00 h01 h10 h11
    simp [cellPhi, hz, cellMass, outcomeMean, armMass, h00, h01, h10, h11]
  · simp [cellPhi, hz, cellVector, vectorMass, vectorArmMass, cellMass,
      outcomeMean, armMass, finTwoEquiv]
    <;> ring
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional_eq_weighted_regression
entries.doc: The equivalent weighted-regression formula under overlap.

entries.statement: ∀ {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Overlap epsilon P → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional P ∈ Set.Icc (-1) 1
entries.source:
/-- Shows that ate Functional mem interval lies in the stated set or interval. -/
lemma ateFunctional_mem_interval {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ} (P : DiscreteLaw d)
    (h : Overlap epsilon P) : ateFunctional P ∈ Set.Icc (-1 : ℝ) 1 := by
  rw [ateFunctional_eq_weighted_regression P h]
  have hsum : ∑ k : Fin d, cellMass P k = 1 := by
    have htotal : ∑ z : Obs d, (P.pmf z).toReal = 1 := by
      simpa using (PMF.integral_eq_sum P.pmf (fun _ : Obs d => (1 : ℝ))).symm
    calc
      ∑ k : Fin d, cellMass P k = ∑ z : Obs d, (P.pmf z).toReal := by
        simp [cellMass, jointMass, Fintype.sum_prod_type]
      _ = 1 := htotal
  have habs :
      |∑ k : Fin d, cellMass P k *
          (outcomeMean P true k - outcomeMean P false k)| ≤ 1 := by
    calc
      |∑ k : Fin d, cellMass P k *
          (outcomeMean P true k - outcomeMean P false k)| ≤
          ∑ k : Fin d, |cellMass P k *
            (outcomeMean P true k - outcomeMean P false k)| :=
        Finset.abs_sum_le_sum_abs _ _
      _ ≤ ∑ k : Fin d, cellMass P k := by
        apply Finset.sum_le_sum
        intro k _
        have hp := cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k
        have hm1 := outcomeMean_mem_unitInterval P true k
        have hm0 := outcomeMean_mem_unitInterval P false k
        rcases hm1 with ⟨hm1lo, hm1hi⟩
        rcases hm0 with ⟨hm0lo, hm0hi⟩
        rw [abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg hp.1]
        have hdiff : |outcomeMean P true k - outcomeMean P false k| ≤ 1 := by
          rw [abs_le]
          constructor <;> linarith
        exact mul_le_of_le_one_right hp.1 hdiff
      _ = 1 := hsum
  exact abs_le.mp habs
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ateFunctional_mem_interval
entries.doc: Shows that ate Functional mem interval lies in the stated set or interval.

entries.statement: ∀ {epsilon : ℝ}, CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ZengOneArmMinimaxLower epsilon → 0 < epsilon → epsilon < 1 / 2 → ∃ a_epsilon b_epsilon N_epsilon, 0 < a_epsilon ∧ 0 < b_epsilon ∧ ∀ (n d : ℕ), 0 < d → N_epsilon ≤ n → ↑d ≤ b_epsilon * ↑n * Real.log ↑n → a_epsilon * CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.minimaxRate n d ≤ CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.minimaxRisk n d epsilon
entries.source:
/-- Transfers the cited control-zero one-arm converse to the unrestricted ATE
minimax problem by restriction of the law class. -/
lemma ate_lower_bound_transfer {epsilon : ℝ}
    (hZeng : ZengOneArmMinimaxLower epsilon) (he0 : 0 < epsilon)
    (he1 : epsilon < 1 / 2) :
    ∃ a_epsilon b_epsilon : ℝ, ∃ N_epsilon : ℕ,
      0 < a_epsilon ∧ 0 < b_epsilon ∧
      ∀ n d : ℕ, 0 < d → N_epsilon ≤ n →
        (d : ℝ) ≤ b_epsilon * n * Real.log n →
        a_epsilon * minimaxRate n d ≤ minimaxRisk n d epsilon := by
  rcases hZeng ⟨he0, he1⟩ with
    ⟨a_epsilon, b_epsilon, N_epsilon, ha, hb, hLower⟩
  refine ⟨a_epsilon, b_epsilon, N_epsilon, ha, hb, ?_⟩
  intro n d hd_pos hn hd_range
  exact (hLower n d hd_pos hn hd_range).trans
    (oneArmMinimaxRisk_le_minimaxRisk n d epsilon)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ate_lower_bound_transfer
entries.doc:
Transfers the cited control-zero one-arm converse to the unrestricted ATE
minimax problem by restriction of the law class.

entries.statement: ∀ {I : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype I] [inst_1 : DecidableEq I] {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ} (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d), CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Overlap epsilon P → 0 < epsilon → ∀ (k : Fin d) (a : Bool), 0 < CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k → (∫ (z : I → CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.Obs d), ↑(CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.indexSet z (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.categorySet k)).card ^ 2 * if 0 < (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.indexSet z (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.categoryArmSet k a)).card then (↑(CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.indexSet z (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.categoryArmSet k a)).card)⁻¹ else 0 ∂MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.obsLaw P) ≤ 2 * ↑(Fintype.card I) * CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.cellMass P k / epsilon
entries.source:
/-- Specialization of the nested-count theorem to one discrete-ATE category
and either treatment arm.  Overlap replaces the arm probability denominator
by the uniform lower bound epsilon. -/
lemma ate_nested_count_ratio_integral_le {I : Type*} [Fintype I]
    [DecidableEq I] {d : ℕ} {epsilon : ℝ}
    (P : DiscreteLaw d) (hOverlap : Overlap epsilon P) (hepsilon : 0 < epsilon)
    (k : Fin d) (a : Bool) (hp : 0 < cellMass P k) :
    ∫ z : I → Obs d, ((indexSet z (categorySet k)).card : ℝ) ^ 2 *
        (if 0 < (indexSet z (categoryArmSet k a)).card then
          ((indexSet z (categoryArmSet k a)).card : ℝ)⁻¹ else 0)
        ∂(Measure.pi (fun _ : I ↦ obsLaw P)) ≤
      2 * (Fintype.card I : ℝ) * cellMass P k / epsilon := by
  have hrhoLower := armPropensity_lower_of_overlap P hOverlap k a hp
  have hrhoPos : 0 < armPropensity P k a := lt_of_lt_of_le hepsilon hrhoLower
  have hbase := nested_count_ratio_integral_le (I := I) (P := obsLaw P)
    (categorySet k) (categoryArmSet k a) MeasurableSet.of_discrete
    MeasurableSet.of_discrete (categoryArmSet_subset_categorySet k a)
    (cellMass P k) (armPropensity P k a)
    (obsLaw_categorySet_mass P k)
    ((obsLaw_categoryArmSet_mass P k a).trans
      (armMass_eq_cellMass_mul_armPropensity P k a hp))
    (obsLaw_categoryArm_diff_mass P k a hp)
    (obsLaw_categorySet_compl_mass P k)
    (cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k).1 (cellMass_mem_unitInterval P k).2
    hrhoPos (armPropensity_mem_unitInterval P k a).2
  calc
    _ ≤ 2 * (Fintype.card I : ℝ) * cellMass P k /
        armPropensity P k a := hbase
    _ ≤ 2 * (Fintype.card I : ℝ) * cellMass P k / epsilon := by
      exact div_le_div_of_nonneg_left (by positivity) hepsilon hrhoLower
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.ate_nested_count_ratio_integral_le
entries.doc:
Specialization of the nested-count theorem to one discrete-ATE category
and either treatment arm.  Overlap replaces the arm probability denominator
by the uniform lower bound epsilon.

entries.statement: {d : ℕ} → (P : CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.DiscreteLaw d) → Causalean.Stat.OneShotSplit (Causalean.Stat.iidSample_infinitePi (CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.obsLaw P))
entries.source:
/-- The deterministic half split used by the estimator, packaged in the
generic one-shot independence interface. -/
noncomputable def balancedOneShotSplit {d : ℕ} (P : DiscreteLaw d) :
    Causalean.Stat.OneShotSplit
      (Causalean.Stat.iidSample_infinitePi (obsLaw P)) where
  n₁ n := n / 2
  bound n := Nat.div_le_self n 2
  grow := Nat.tendsto_div_const_atTop (by norm_num)
  cogrow := by
    show Filter.Tendsto (fun n : ℕ ↦ n - n / 2) Filter.atTop Filter.atTop
    apply Filter.tendsto_atTop_mono
      (f := fun n : ℕ ↦ n / 2) (g := fun n ↦ n - n / 2)
    · intro n
      omega
    · exact Nat.tendsto_div_const_atTop (by norm_num)
entries.name: CausalSmith.Stat.DiscreteAteMinimaxLoggap.balancedOneShotSplit
entries.doc:
The deterministic half split used by the estimator, packaged in the
generic one-shot independence interface.
